---
title: "36-671 Final Project"
author: "Ellie Huang, Nikita Jayaprakash, Annie Wang, Aishani Pradhan, Steve Wong"
date: "2026-04-19"
output: pdf_document
---

Research question: 

# Finding, downloading and cleaning up relevant time series data

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
library(here)
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

file_path <- here("data", "gasoline_data.xls")
brent_path <- here("data", "brent_crude_monthly_1993_present.csv")
```

```{r import-and-clean, message = FALSE}
# Load gasoline data
# skip = 2 because row 1 = title, row 2 = source key, row 3 = actual variable names

us_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "Data 1", skip = 2) |>
  rename(Date = 1, US_All_Grades = 2) |>
  mutate(Date = format(as.Date(Date), "%Y-%m"))

region_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "Data 2", skip = 2) |>
  rename(
    Date = 1,
    East_Coast = 2,
    New_England = 3,
    Central_Atlantic = 4,
    Lower_Atlantic = 5,
    Midwest = 6,
    Gulf_Coast = 7,
    Rocky_Mountain = 8,
    West_Coast = 9,
    West_Coast_ex_CA = 10
  ) |>
  mutate(Date = format(as.Date(Date), "%Y-%m"))

# Join national + the five regional series we use, restrict to common date range
gas_complete <- us_data |>
  select(Date, US_All_Grades) |>
  left_join(
    region_data |> select(Date, East_Coast, Midwest, Gulf_Coast, Rocky_Mountain, West_Coast),
    by = "Date"
  ) |>
  drop_na()

cat("Common gasoline date range:", min(gas_complete$Date), "to", max(gas_complete$Date), "\n")
cat("Observations:", nrow(gas_complete), "\n")

# Load Brent crude oil data
brent_data <- read_csv(brent_path) |>
  rename(Date = DATE, Brent = Brent_Price_USD) |>
  mutate(
    Date = format(as.Date(Date), "%Y-%m"),
    Brent = as.numeric(Brent)
  )
```

```{r gasoline-ts}
# Convert gasoline series to ts objects
# Pull start date once and reuse for all regional series
start_vec <- c(
  as.numeric(substr(min(gas_complete$Date), 1, 4)),
  as.numeric(substr(min(gas_complete$Date), 6, 7))
)

us_ts <- ts(gas_complete$US_All_Grades, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
east_ts <- ts(gas_complete$East_Coast, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
midwest_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Midwest, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
gulf_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Gulf_Coast, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
rocky_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Rocky_Mountain, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
west_ts <- ts(gas_complete$West_Coast, start = start_vec, frequency = 12)
```

# Exploratory Data Analysis

```{r}
# Plot national and regional gasoline prices
plot(us_ts,
     main = "U.S. All Grades Gasoline Prices",
     xlab = "Year", ylab = "Dollars per Gallon")

ts.plot(east_ts, midwest_ts, gulf_ts, rocky_ts, west_ts,
        col = 1:5, lty = 1,
        main = "Regional Monthly Gasoline Prices",
        xlab = "Year", ylab = "Dollars per Gallon")
legend("topleft",
       legend = c("East Coast", "Midwest", "Gulf Coast", "Rocky Mountain", "West Coast"),
       col = 1:5, lty = 1, bty = "n")
```

```{r brent-plot}
# Plot Brent crude oil prices
brent_data |>
  mutate(Date_plot = as.Date(paste0(Date, "-01"))) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Date_plot, y = Brent)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Monthly Brent Crude Oil Prices",
       x = "Date", y = "USD per Barrel") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r gas-brent-join}
# Join gasoline and Brent crude, restrict to overlapping months
gas_brent <- gas_complete |>
  select(Date, US_All_Grades) |>
  inner_join(brent_data, by = "Date") |>
  drop_na()

cat("Common gas-Brent date range:", min(gas_brent$Date), "to", max(gas_brent$Date), "\n")
cat("Observations:", nrow(gas_brent), "\n")

# Convert to ts objects on the shared date range
start_brent <- c(
  as.numeric(substr(min(gas_brent$Date), 1, 4)),
  as.numeric(substr(min(gas_brent$Date), 6, 7))
)

gas_ts_brent <- ts(gas_brent$US_All_Grades, start = start_brent, frequency = 12)
brent_ts <- ts(gas_brent$Brent, start = start_brent, frequency = 12)
```
